description: Tests that the stack depth skill correctly runs asm-analyze stack-depth, interprets the JSON output, and reports worst-case depth, call paths, per-function frames, warnings, and optional budget verdicts. All functions are compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
note: Set $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT to your local BLE project root before running.

evals.prompt: Analyze the stack depth for GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/examples/rtos/LP_EM_CC2340R5/ble5stack/basic_ble/freertos/ticlang/basic_ble.out. No stack budget — just show me the worst-case depth and the call path.
evals.expected_output: Runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --elf-path and --entry-functions GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent. Reports worst-case depth in bytes, the worst-case call path as a chain of function names, average depth, and per-function frame sizes along the worst path. Does not report a verdict since no budget was provided.

assertions.text: Response runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --elf-path pointing to basic_ble.out and --entry-functions GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent
assertions.description: Must use the stack-depth subcommand with the correct entry function

assertions.text: Response reports a worst-case depth in bytes (a specific number, not fabricated)
assertions.description: The core output: total accumulated stack bytes along the deepest path

assertions.text: Response shows a worst-case call path as a chain of function names (more than one function)
assertions.description: The path must show the actual chain from entry to deepest leaf

assertions.text: Response reports per-function frame sizes for functions along the worst path
assertions.description: Each function in the path should show its individual frame contribution

assertions.text: Response does NOT report a pass/fail verdict or usage percentage
assertions.description: No budget was provided so no threshold check should appear

evals.prompt: Run stack depth analysis on GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/examples/rtos/LP_EM_CC2340R5/ble5stack/basic_ble/freertos/ticlang/basic_ble.out with a stack budget of 768 bytes and a 50% threshold.
evals.expected_output: Runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --elf-path, --entry-functions, --stack-budget 768, and --threshold 50. Reports worst-case depth, usage as a percentage of 768 bytes, and a PASS or FAIL verdict against the 50% threshold (384 bytes). Also reports the worst-case path and per-function frames.

assertions.text: Response runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --stack-budget 768 and --threshold 50
assertions.description: Budget and threshold flags must be passed to enable the verdict

assertions.text: Response reports usage as a percentage of the 768-byte budget
assertions.description: Usage percentage is required when a budget is provided

assertions.text: Response includes a PASS or FAIL verdict
assertions.description: The verdict compares worst-case depth against 50% of 768 bytes (384 bytes)

assertions.text: Response shows the worst-case call path and per-function frame sizes
assertions.description: Full reporting is still required alongside the verdict

evals.prompt: Analyze stack depth for all root functions in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/examples/rtos/LP_EM_CC2340R5/ble5stack/basic_ble/freertos/ticlang/basic_ble.out. Don't specify entry functions — let the tool auto-detect them.
evals.expected_output: Runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --elf-path but without --entry-functions, letting the tool auto-detect root functions. Reports per-function results for multiple entry points, each with worst-case depth, path, and frame sizes.

assertions.text: Response runs asm-analyze stack-depth without --entry-functions to auto-detect roots
assertions.description: Auto-detection mode should be used when no entry functions are specified

assertions.text: Response reports results for more than one function
assertions.description: A real ELF has multiple root functions; the tool should find and report them

assertions.text: Each reported function has a worst-case depth and path
assertions.description: Per-function reporting is required even in multi-function mode

evals.prompt: Analyze stack depth for osal_msg_send in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/examples/rtos/LP_EM_CC2340R5/ble5stack/basic_ble/freertos/ticlang/basic_ble.out. I suspect there may be recursive calls in the chain. Use --max-recursion-depth 3.
evals.expected_output: Runs asm-analyze stack-depth with --entry-functions osal_msg_send and --max-recursion-depth 3. If recursion is detected, reports a warning about the recursive call and notes the depth is bounded to 3 iterations. If no recursion is found, reports the depth normally. Either way, the tool should handle the flag and report clearly.

assertions.description: The recursion depth flag must be passed through

assertions.text: Response reports the worst-case depth and path for osal_msg_send
assertions.description: Core analysis must complete regardless of recursion

assertions.text: If recursion is detected, response mentions it explicitly as a warning
assertions.description: Recursion warnings are critical for stack safety — they must be surfaced
